metric: 1.1 Program Executability: The system can start normally and enter the main menu interface.
description: 1. Act: Execute the program start command (like python src/main_console.py) in the shell.\n2. Assert: Observe whether the program successfully starts and displays a clear main menu with functional options.
expected_output: The program successfully starts and displays a main menu with the following options: 1. Add Member Information 2. Query Member Information by Relative 3. Query Basic Member Information 4. Statistics of Member Information 5. Display in Tree Form 6. Exit System

metric: 2.1.1.1 Member Information Entry: Date Format Verification - Birth/Death date must match the YYYYMMDD format.
description: 1. Arrange: Prepare a member information where the birth date or death date is filled in an illegal format (such as '1990-01-01' or '900101').\n2. Act: Select the add new member function and attempt to submit the prepared information.\n3. Assert: Observe whether the system prompts a date format error (such as "Date format should be YYYYMMDD") and prevents submission.
expected_output: The system displays an error message: 'Date format should be YYYYMMDD', and prevents data submission

metric: 2.1.1.2 Member Information Entry: Value Range Verification - Height must be between 50-300 cm.
description: 1. Arrange: Prepare a member information where height is filled with an illegal value (such as 40 or 350).\n2. Act: Select the add new member function and attempt to submit the prepared information.\n3. Assert: Observe whether the system prompts a height range error (such as "Height should be between 50-300 cm") and prevents submission.
expected_output: The system displays an error message: 'Height should be between 50-300 cm', and prevents data submission

metric: 2.1.1.3 Member Information Entry: Education Enumeration Verification - Must match predefined education values.
description: 1. Arrange: Prepare a member information where the education level is filled with an illegal value (such as 'Postdoctoral').\n2. Act: Select the add new member function and attempt to submit the prepared information.\n3. Assert: Observe whether the system prompts an education value error (such as "Education must be one of Primary/Middle/High School/Bachelor/Masters/Doctorate") and prevents submission.
expected_output: The system displays an error message: 'Education must be one of Primary/Middle/High School/Bachelor/Masters/Doctorate', and prevents data submission

metric: 2.1.1.4 Member Information Entry: Relationship Logic Verification - Specified relatives must exist in the system.
description: 1. Arrange: Prepare a member information specifying a relative's name who does not exist in the system.\n2. Act: Select the add new member function and attempt to submit the prepared information.\n3. Assert: Observe whether the system prompts that the relative does not exist (such as "The specified relative XXX does not exist in the system") and prevents submission.
expected_output: The system displays an error message: 'The specified relative XXX does not exist in the system', and prevents data submission

metric: 2.1.2.1 Member Information Storage: CSV File Creation and Data Writing.
description: 1. Arrange: Ensure there is no data.csv file in the project directory.\n2. Act: Run the program for the first time and enter a member's information.\n3. Assert: Check whether a data.csv file is generated in the project directory and contains the newly entered member information.
expected_output: The data.csv file was successfully created, and the file content includes the newly entered member information

metric: 2.1.2.2 Member Information Storage: Incremental Storage Mode - New member information is appended.
description: 1. Arrange: Ensure there is already a data.csv file in the project directory containing at least one member.\n2. Act: Enter another new member.\n3. Assert: Check whether the data.csv file appended the new member information while retaining the original data.
expected_output: The data.csv file successfully appended the new member information while retaining the original data

metric: 2.2.1.1 Tree Structure Construction: Member Class Definition and Root Node Setup.
description: 1. Arrange: Enter at least one member.\n2. Act: Check the generated family tree structure.\n3. Assert: Confirm that the Member object contains idx, kids, spouse attributes, and the first member is set as the root node (relation type -1).
expected_output: The Member object contains idx, kids, spouse attributes, and the first member is set as the root node (relation type -1)

metric: 2.2.1.2 Tree Structure Construction: Spousal Relation Two-way Establishment and Child Sharing.
description: 1. Arrange: Enter a married couple and their children.\n2. Act: Check the generated family tree structure.\n3. Assert: Confirm whether the spouse attributes of both parties correctly point to each other and whether their kids' lists are consistent and contain children.
expected_output: The spouse attributes of both parties correctly point to each other, and their kids' lists are consistent and include child indices

metric: 2.2.1.3 Tree Structure Construction: Parent-child Relationship Establishment and Index Lookup.
description: 1. Arrange: Enter a parent-child relationship.\n2. Act: Check the generated family tree structure and attempt to find a member by name.\n3. Assert: Confirm whether the parent's kids list contains the child's index and whether the system can properly locate the member's position in the family list by name.
expected_output: The parent's kids list contains the child's index, and the system can properly locate the member's position in the family list by name

metric: 2.2.2.1a Relationship Query: Parent Search Algorithm and Generation Control - 1st Generation Parent Query
description: 1. Arrange: Build a family structure containing at least a parent-child relationship.\n2. Act: Choose the relationship query function, then select "Parent Query", specifying the target member and the generation to query as 1.\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned result correctly includes the target member's parent information.
expected_output: The system correctly returns the target member's parent information

metric: 2.2.2.1b Relationship Query: Parent Search Algorithm and Generation Control - 2nd Generation Parent Query (Grandparents)
description: 1. Arrange: Build a family structure containing at least a three-generation relationship of grandparents, parents, and children.\n2. Act: Choose the relationship query function, then select "Parent Query", specifying the target member and the generation to query as 2.\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned result correctly includes the target member's grandparent information.
expected_output: The system correctly returns the target member's grandparent information

metric: 2.2.2.2 Relationship Query: Parent Search Algorithm and Gender Filtering.
description: 1. Arrange: Build a family structure with both male and female parents.\n2. Act: Choose the relationship query function, then select "Parent Query", specify the target member, query generation as 1, and add gender filtering conditions (such as querying only males).\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned results only contain parent members that meet the criteria.
expected_output: The system only returns parent members that meet the gender filtering criteria

metric: 2.2.2.3a Relationship Query: Descendants Search Algorithm and Across-generation Query - 1st Generation Descendant Query
description: 1. Arrange: Build a family structure containing a parent-child relationship.\n2. Act: Choose the relationship query function, then select "Descendant Query", specifying the target member and the generation to query as 1.\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned result correctly includes the target member's child information.
expected_output: The system correctly returns the target member's child information

metric: 2.2.2.3b Relationship Query: Descendants Search Algorithm and Across-generation Query - 2nd Generation Descendant Query (Grandchildren)
description: 1. Arrange: Build a family structure containing a three-generation relationship including grandparents, parents, and children.\n2. Act: Choose the relationship query function, then select "Descendant Query", specifying the target member and the generation to query as 2.\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned result correctly includes the target member's grandchildren information.
expected_output: The system correctly returns the target member's grandchildren information

metric: 2.2.2.4 Relationship Query: Descendants Search Algorithm and Gender Filtering.
description: 1. Arrange: Build a family structure with multiple children (both male and female).\n2. Act: Choose the relationship query function, then select "Descendant Query", specify the target member, query generation as 1, and add gender filtering conditions (such as querying only females).\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned results only contain descendants that meet the criteria.
expected_output: The system only returns descendant members that meet the gender filtering criteria

metric: 2.2.2.5 Relationship Query: Spouse Query Algorithm and Exception Handling.
description: 1. Arrange: Prepare a married member and an unmarried member.\n2. Act: Perform spouse query on the married member and on the unmarried member.\n3. Assert: Check whether the spouse query of the married member returns spouse information; whether the query of the unmarried member returns a "No spouse found" or similar prompt.
expected_output: The married member query returns spouse information; unmarried member query returns 'No spouse found, query failed' prompt

metric: 2.3.1.1a Multidimensional Query: Exact Name Matching Query
description: 1. Arrange: Ensure there is a member named "Zhang San" in the system.\n2. Act: Choose the information retrieval function, then select "Query by Name", and enter "Zhang San".\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned result correctly includes the information of "Zhang San".
expected_output: The system correctly returns complete information of the member named 'Zhang San'

metric: 2.3.1.1b Multidimensional Query: Fuzzy Place of Birth Matching Query
description: 1. Arrange: Ensure there is a member with the birthplace "Chaoyang District, Beijing" in the system.\n2. Act: Choose the information retrieval function, then select "Query by Birthplace", and enter "Beijing".\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned result correctly includes the information of the member from "Chaoyang District, Beijing".
expected_output: The system correctly returns all information of members whose birthplace contains 'Beijing'

metric: 2.3.1.1c Multidimensional Query: Exact Birth Date Matching Query
description: 1. Arrange: Ensure there is a member with the birth date "19901212" in the system.\n2. Act: Choose the information retrieval function, then select "Query by Birth Date", and enter "19901212".\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned result correctly includes the information of that member.
expected_output: The system correctly returns complete information of the member with the birth date '19901212'

metric: 2.3.1.1d Multidimensional Query: Education Level Matching Query
description: 1. Arrange: Ensure there is a member with the education level "Bachelor" in the system.\n2. Act: Choose the information retrieval function, then select "Query by Education", and choose "Bachelor".\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned result correctly includes the information of that member.
expected_output: The system correctly returns all members with the education level 'Bachelor'

metric: 2.3.1.1e Multidimensional Query: Key Word Query for Occupation
description: 1. Arrange: Ensure there is a member with the occupation "Software Engineer" in the system.\n2. Act: Choose the information retrieval function, then select "Query by Occupation", and enter "Software".\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned result correctly includes the information of the "Software Engineer" member.
expected_output: The system correctly returns all members with occupations containing the keyword 'Software'

metric: 2.3.1.1f Multidimensional Query: Highest Position Matching Query
description: 1. Arrange: Ensure there is a member with the highest position "Department Manager" in the system.\n2. Act: Choose the information retrieval function, then select "Query by Highest Position", and enter "Department Manager".\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned result correctly includes the information of that member.
expected_output: The system correctly returns all members with the highest position of 'Department Manager'

metric: 2.3.1.1g Multidimensional Query: Gender Classification Query
description: 1. Arrange: Ensure there are male and female members in the system.\n2. Act: Choose the information retrieval function, then select "Query by Gender", and choose "Male".\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned result only includes the information of male members.
expected_output: The system only returns all members with the gender 'Male'

metric: 2.3.1.2 Multidimensional Query: Handling of No Matching Results.
description: 1. Arrange: Prepare a query condition that does not exist in the system.\n2. Act: Perform the query using this condition.\n3. Assert: Check whether the system returns a -1 code or corresponding prompt information (such as "No matching results found").
expected_output: The system returns the prompt 'The member you are searching for does not exist'

metric: 2.5.1.1 Basic Statistics: Average Height Calculation and Data Filtering.
description: 1. Arrange: Ensure there are multiple members in the system, including valid and invalid height data.\n2. Act: Choose the statistical analysis function, then select "Height Statistics".\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned average height is correct and whether invalid data (like zero values) is filtered out.
expected_output: The system correctly calculates and displays the average height in the format: 'Average height: XXX.X cm', and filters out invalid data

metric: 2.5.1.2 Basic Statistics: Education Level Conversion and Statistics.
description: 1. Arrange: Ensure there are multiple members in the system with different educational backgrounds.\n2. Act: Choose the statistical analysis function, then select "Education Statistics".\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned average education level, highest education level, and lowest education level are correct, and whether they are based on numerical level conversion.
expected_output: The system correctly displays education statistics, including average education, highest education, and lowest education information

metric: 2.5.2.1 Population Statistics: Gender Ratio Statistics and Simplification.
description: 1. Arrange: Ensure there are male and female members in the system.\n2. Act: Choose the statistical analysis function, then select "Gender Ratio Statistics".\n3. Assert: Check whether the returned male-to-female ratio is correct and whether it is simplified (such as simplifying 6:4 to 3:2).
expected_output: The system correctly displays gender ratio statistics in the format: 'Male to Female Ratio: X:Y', and it is simplified

metric: 2.6.1.1 Tree Display: Text Tree Structure and Hierarchical Indentation.
description: 1. Arrange: Ensure there are multiple generations of family members in the system.\n2. Act: Choose the genealogy display function.\n3. Assert: Check whether the output is a correct text tree structure and whether each generation is appropriately indented (such as 2 spaces).
expected_output: The system outputs a correct text tree structure, with appropriate indentations for each generation (2 spaces), clear hierarchical relationships, and members displayed in the '--Name' format, with spouse names following immediately
